why: terminology unification: one capability-internal decision structure carried three names (logic graph, decision graph, decision tree) and was defined in none of them; the corpus glossary had no entry at all. medium blast — the rename lands in four shipped governance skills that police every node in the corpus, and renames a mandated spec section heading, but introduces no behavior and narrows nothing. no Clearance floor: the spec-format node is README-only so the four-section contract is frozen in no scenario, and the single .feature hit is a comment carrying no scenario delta. auto-spec because the spec gate judges a rename with no new contract, while the impl gate is owner-ratified — a half-applied rename ships two names for one concept, which is the exact defect being fixed, so residual-hit verification wants the owner's eye
approach: exhaustive classified sweep first — grep the three terms, then sweep the pronoun/indirect references (this graph, sub-graph, grows the graph around it) that a term-grep cannot find
rename the concept noun corpus-wide to control-flow graph (CFG), first-use-parenthetical per document
rename the mandated section heading ## Logic -> ## Control Flow across governance, scaffold, and the two live corpus instances
spec-format README diagram-kind list: decision tree -> control-flow, kept as a diagram kind, list unrestructured
glossary entry for control-flow graph (CFG) plus three Retired terms rows, same commit as the rename
protect the false-positive classes: mission-graph, work-graph, the website's process Control Flow page, universal-plugin's unrelated decision tree, historical plan briefs

why.floor: none — freeze preserved and proven, not asserted: gherkin-cli structural diff on the one touched .feature reports modified 0, removed 0, unchanged 15, addOnly true, because the only hit was a comment carrying no scenario delta; no narrowing, so no Clearance
why.blast: medium — four shipped governance skills that police every node in the corpus, one gate script comment, six corpus spec documents, and the mandated section heading; no behavior added, nothing narrowed
why.novelty: low — a terminology unification with the name settled upstream; no new autonomy floor and no new contract, so the spec gate had no new scenario to freeze
why.confidence: high — the section heading was checked against the gate scripts before renaming: check-spec-state requires only Use Cases for a behavioral node and check-suite indexes only Scenario map, so no lint keys on the old heading. Discovery ran twice by different methods, and a whitespace-normalized sweep found two occurrences wrapped across a line break that every single-line grep missed, which is the failure mode this CR exists to prevent. Residual in-scope hits now zero. Root verify 34/34

why.floor: none — freeze preserved and proven by structural diff, not asserted; nothing narrowed, no Clearance
why.blast: medium — four shipped governance skills, one gate-script comment, six corpus documents, and the mandated section heading; terminology only, no behavior added
why.novelty: low — no new autonomy floor; the name was settled upstream and the two scope rulings were taken from the owner before any edit landed
why.confidence: high — a cold judge re-derived its own oracle across six axes and returned substance PASS on all of them, failing only prose rewrap, which was remediated and recorded as a judge-iteration correction. Discovery ran twice by independent methods and the whitespace-normalized pass found two occurrences wrapped across a line break that every single-line sweep missed, including the one behind the originating site table. Residual in-scope hits zero. The heading rename was cleared against the gate scripts before it landed. Root verify 34/34
